audit_status.review_note: Reviewed for Isaac sending Jacob to Paddan-Aram, blessing of Abraham, Esau's additional marriage, Jacob's ladder/stairway dream, Bethel naming, stone pillar, and vow/tithe language.

verses.restored: Isaac called Jacob, blessed him, commanded him, and said to him, You shall not take a woman from the daughters of Canaan.

familiar.text: And Isaac called Jacob, and blessed him, and charged him...

verses.restored: Rise, go to Paddan-Aram, to the house of Bethuel your mother’s father, and take for yourself from there a woman from the daughters of Laban, your mother’s brother.

familiar.text: Arise, go to Padanaram, to the house of Bethuel thy mother's father...

verses.restored: May El Shaddai bless you, make you fruitful, and multiply you, so that you become an assembly of peoples.

familiar.text: And God Almighty bless thee, and make thee fruitful...

notes.title: El Shaddai
notes.body: Isaac invokes the covenant title associated with Abraham’s renaming and fruitfulness.

verses.restored: May he give you the blessing of Abraham, to you and to your seed with you, so that you may possess the land of your sojournings, which the Divine gave to Abraham.

familiar.text: And give thee the blessing of Abraham, to thee, and to thy seed...

notes.title: Blessing of Abraham
notes.body: The covenant blessing is now explicitly passed to Jacob.

verses.restored: Isaac sent Jacob away, and he went to Paddan-Aram, to Laban son of Bethuel the Aramean, brother of Rebekah, mother of Jacob and Esau.

familiar.text: And Isaac sent away Jacob: and he went to Padanaram unto Laban...

verses.restored: Esau saw that Isaac had blessed Jacob and sent him to Paddan-Aram to take a woman from there, and that as he blessed him he commanded him, saying, You shall not take a woman from the daughters of Canaan,

familiar.text: When Esau saw that Isaac had blessed Jacob...

verses.restored: and that Jacob had listened to his father and his mother and had gone to Paddan-Aram,

familiar.text: And that Jacob obeyed his father and his mother...

verses.restored: Esau saw that the daughters of Canaan were harmful in the eyes of Isaac his father.

familiar.text: And Esau seeing that the daughters of Canaan pleased not Isaac his father;

verses.restored: So Esau went to Ishmael and took Mahalath, daughter of Ishmael son of Abraham, sister of Nebaioth, in addition to his women, to be his woman.

familiar.text: Then went Esau unto Ishmael, and took unto the wives which he had Mahalath...

notes.title: Esau responds by marriage
notes.body: Esau tries to address his parents’ displeasure by marrying within Abraham’s wider family through Ishmael.

verses.restored: Jacob went out from Beersheba and went toward Haran.

familiar.text: And Jacob went out from Beersheba, and went toward Haran.

verses.restored: He came upon a place and spent the night there, because the sun had gone down. He took one of the stones of the place, set it at his head, and lay down in that place.

familiar.text: And he lighted upon a certain place, and tarried there all night...

notes.title: The place
notes.body: Maqom, place, repeats and becomes sacred through encounter.

verses.restored: He dreamed, and look, a stairway was set on the earth, its head reaching the heavens, and look, messengers of the Divine were ascending and descending on it.

familiar.text: And he dreamed, and behold a ladder set up on the earth...

notes.title: Stairway/ladder
notes.body: Sullam may be ladder, stairway, or ramp. It joins earth and heavens in Jacob’s dream.

verses.restored: And look, the Eternal stood above it and said, I am the Eternal, Divine of Abraham your father and Divine of Isaac. The land on which you are lying, to you I will give it and to your seed.

familiar.text: And, behold, the LORD stood above it, and said...

verses.restored: Your seed shall be like the dust of the earth. You shall spread westward, eastward, northward, and southward, and in you and in your seed all the families of the ground shall be blessed.

familiar.text: And thy seed shall be as the dust of the earth...

verses.restored: Look, I am with you, and I will keep you wherever you go. I will return you to this ground, for I will not leave you until I have done what I have spoken to you.

familiar.text: And, behold, I am with thee, and will keep thee in all places...

notes.title: I am with you
notes.body: The promise centers presence, protection, return, and completion.

verses.restored: Jacob awoke from his sleep and said, Surely the Eternal is in this place, and I did not know.

familiar.text: And Jacob awaked out of his sleep, and he said, Surely the LORD is in this place...

verses.restored: He was afraid and said, How awe-filled is this place! This is none other than the house of the Divine, and this is the gate of the heavens.

familiar.text: And he was afraid, and said, How dreadful is this place!...

notes.title: Awe-filled
notes.body: Nora can mean fearful, awesome, or awe-filled. The fear is sacred awe.

verses.restored: Jacob rose early in the morning, took the stone that he had set at his head, set it up as a pillar, and poured oil on its head.

familiar.text: And Jacob rose up early in the morning, and took the stone...

verses.restored: He called the name of that place Bethel, though Luz was the name of the city at first.

familiar.text: And he called the name of that place Bethel...

notes.title: Bethel
notes.body: Bethel means House of God/the Divine.

verses.restored: Jacob vowed a vow, saying, If the Divine will be with me, keep me on this way that I am going, give me bread to eat and clothing to wear,

familiar.text: And Jacob vowed a vow, saying, If God will be with me...

notes.title: Jacob’s vow
notes.body: Jacob responds conditionally to the unconditional promise, asking for presence, protection, bread, clothing, and return.

verses.restored: and I return in peace to my father’s house, then the Eternal shall be my Divine.

familiar.text: So that I come again to my father's house in peace; then shall the LORD be my God:

verses.restored: This stone that I have set up as a pillar shall be the house of the Divine, and of all that you give me I will surely give a tenth to you.

familiar.text: And this stone, which I have set for a pillar, shall be God's house...
